name: HitlClient
description: High-level client for HITL lifecycle management. Handles task creation, polling, artifact download, refresh loops, and task closure.

parameters.name: agentic_client
parameters.description: boto3 ElasticGumbyAgenticService client

parameters.name: request_context
parameters.description: Snake_case dataclass or camelCase dict with job_id, workspace_id, agent_instance_id, authorization_token

parameters.name: poll_interval_seconds
parameters.description: Seconds between status polls

parameters.name: poll_timeout_minutes
parameters.description: Max minutes to wait for human submission (2 hours default)

example:
from elastic_gumby_hitl_component_python_sdk import HitlClient, RequestContext
import os

request_context = RequestContext(
    job_id=os.environ['JOB_ID'],
    workspace_id=os.environ['WORKSPACE_ID'],
    agent_instance_id=os.environ['AGENT_INSTANCE_ID'],
    authorization_token=os.environ['AUTHORIZATION_TOKEN'],
)
client = HitlClient(agentic_client, request_context)

name: HitlClient.create_and_start_task
description: Create and start a HITL task. Calls both CreateHitlTask AND StartHitlTask API operations in sequence.
signature: create_and_start_task(ux_component_id, title, description, hitl_request_artifact=None, first_in_chain=True, *, severity=Severity.STANDARD, hitl_task_type=HitlTaskType.NORMAL, blocking_type=BlockingType.BLOCKING, category=Category.REGULAR, step_id=None, tag=None, expired_at=None, idempotency_token=None) -> str

parameters.name: ux_component_id
parameters.description: UX component to render (e.g. 'TextInput', 'DynamicHITLRenderEngine', 'AutoForm', 'GeneralConnector', 'FileUploadComponent', 'TableComponent', 'MarkdownRendererComponent', 'SelectDropdownComponent', 'FeedbackInput')

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: Displayed in the project plan (max 1024 chars)

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Displayed in the execution log (max 1024 chars)

parameters.name: hitl_request_artifact
parameters.description: Artifact containing data for the UX component. Dict must have 'artifactId' key.

parameters.name: first_in_chain
parameters.description: True if first task in chain, False for refresh iterations

parameters.name: severity
parameters.description: Task priority level. CRITICAL requires admin approval.

parameters.name: hitl_task_type
parameters.description: NORMAL for standard tasks, DASHBOARD for read-only dashboard display

parameters.name: blocking_type
parameters.description: BLOCKING pauses workflow until human responds, NON_BLOCKING continues execution

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: REGULAR for standard tasks, TOOL_APPROVAL for tool usage approval

parameters.name: step_id
parameters.description: Job plan step ID to associate this task with

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Logical identifier to group related HITL tasks, commonly used in refresh loops

parameters.name: expired_at
parameters.description: Expiration timestamp. If not provided, expires when agent stops.

parameters.name: idempotency_token
parameters.description: Token for idempotent task creation

example:
task_id = client.create_and_start_task(
    ux_component_id='DynamicHITLRenderEngine',
    title='Review Migration Plan',
    description='Please review the proposed migration plan',
    hitl_request_artifact={'artifactId': artifact_id},
    severity=Severity.STANDARD,
    blocking_type=BlockingType.BLOCKING,
)

name: HitlClient.get_task
description: Get the current state of a HITL task.

parameters.name: hitl_task_id
parameters.description: The HITL task ID to retrieve

returns.description: HitlTask with hitl_task_id, hitl_task_status, and human_artifact (if submitted)

example:
task = client.get_task(task_id)
print(task.hitl_task_status)  # e.g. HitlTaskStatus.SUBMITTED

name: HitlClient.wait_for_human_submission
description: Wait for the human to submit their response. Blocks until task reaches SUBMITTED status. Delegates to poll_until_status.

parameters.name: hitl_task_id
parameters.description: The HITL task ID to wait for

returns.description: HitlTask with SUBMITTED status and human_artifact.artifact_id

raises.description: If task reaches a terminal status (CANCELLED, CLOSED, CLOSED_PENDING_NEXT_TASK) before SUBMITTED
If polling timeout is exceeded (default 2 hours)

example:
task = client.wait_for_human_submission(task_id)
human_artifact_id = task.human_artifact.artifact_id

name: HitlClient.poll_until_status
description: Poll until a HITL task reaches a specific status. Used internally by wait_for_human_submission but can target any status.

parameters.name: hitl_task_id
parameters.description: The HITL task ID to poll

parameters.name: target_status
parameters.description: The status to wait for (e.g. HitlTaskStatus.SUBMITTED, HitlTaskStatus.DELIVERED)

returns.description: HitlTask when target status is reached

raises.description: If task reaches a terminal status before target

example: task = client.poll_until_status(task_id, HitlTaskStatus.DELIVERED)

name: HitlClient.close_task
description: Close a HITL task with a specified closure type.

parameters.name: hitl_task_id
parameters.description: The HITL task ID to close

parameters.name: closure_type
parameters.description: CLOSED (task completed), CLOSED_PENDING_NEXT_TASK (another task follows, used in refresh loops), or CANCELLED (task was cancelled)

example:
# Normal close:
client.close_task(task_id, ClosureType.CLOSED)

# Refresh loop (more tasks follow):
client.close_task(task_id, ClosureType.CLOSED_PENDING_NEXT_TASK)

name: HitlClient.execute_with_refresh
description: Execute HITL with automatic refresh loop. Handles the full lifecycle: create task -> poll -> download artifact -> check QT_REFRESH flag -> close or refresh -> repeat. Automatically manages first_in_chain, artifact download, deserialization, and task closure.

parameters.name: initial_request_fn
parameters.description: Returns dict of create_and_start_task kwargs (ux_component_id, title, description, hitl_request_artifact, etc.)

parameters.name: on_refresh
parameters.description: Takes previous human response dict, returns dict of create_and_start_task kwargs for the next iteration

parameters.name: max_iterations
parameters.description: Maximum refresh iterations before forcing completion

returns.description: Human artifact response as dict (deserialized JSON from the final submission)

raises.description: If no response received or task has no human artifact

name: HitlTaskStatus
description: HITL task status values matching the AWS Transform Agentic API. Inherits from str and Enum.

values.name: CREATED
values.description: Task has been created but not yet started

values.name: AWAITING_HUMAN_INPUT
values.description: Task is waiting for the human to interact

values.name: IN_PROGRESS
values.description: Human is actively working on the task

values.name: SUBMITTED
values.description: Human has submitted their response. Human artifact is available.

values.name: DELIVERED
values.description: Response has been delivered to the agent

values.name: AWAITING_APPROVAL
values.description: Task requires approval (used with CRITICAL severity)

values.name: CANCELLED
values.description: Task was cancelled (terminal status)

values.name: CLOSED
values.description: Task completed normally (terminal status)

values.name: CLOSED_PENDING_NEXT_TASK
values.description: Task completed, another task follows in the chain (terminal status, used in refresh loops)

example:
from elastic_gumby_hitl_component_python_sdk import HitlTaskStatus

if task.hitl_task_status == HitlTaskStatus.SUBMITTED:
    # Human has responded
    pass

name: ClosureType
description: Closure types for HITL tasks. Determines how a task ends.

values.name: CANCELLED
values.description: Task was cancelled before completion

values.name: CLOSED
values.description: Task completed normally, no more tasks in chain

values.name: CLOSED_PENDING_NEXT_TASK
values.description: Task completed, another task follows. MUST use this in refresh loops before creating the next task.

example:
from elastic_gumby_hitl_component_python_sdk import ClosureType

# Normal completion:
client.close_task(task_id, ClosureType.CLOSED)

# Refresh loop (user requested changes):
client.close_task(task_id, ClosureType.CLOSED_PENDING_NEXT_TASK)

name: Severity
description: HITL task severity levels. Affects routing and approval requirements.

values.name: STANDARD
values.description: Normal priority task (default). No special approval needed.

values.name: CRITICAL
values.description: High priority task requiring admin approval before human can interact.

example:
from elastic_gumby_hitl_component_python_sdk import Severity

# Standard task:
client.create_and_start_task(..., severity=Severity.STANDARD)

# Critical task (requires admin approval):
client.create_and_start_task(..., severity=Severity.CRITICAL)

name: HitlTaskType
description: HITL task types determining how the task is displayed.

values.name: NORMAL
values.description: Standard interactive HITL task (default). Human can submit responses.

values.name: DASHBOARD
values.description: Read-only task displayed on the dashboard. No human input captured.

example:
from elastic_gumby_hitl_component_python_sdk import HitlTaskType

# Dashboard (read-only display):
client.create_and_start_task(
    ux_component_id='MarkdownRendererComponent',
    hitl_task_type=HitlTaskType.DASHBOARD,
    ...)

name: BlockingType
description: HITL task blocking behavior. Controls whether the agent workflow pauses.

values.name: BLOCKING
values.description: Task blocks workflow until human completes it (default). Agent waits.

values.name: NON_BLOCKING
values.description: Task does not block workflow. Agent continues executing while human works.

example:
from elastic_gumby_hitl_component_python_sdk import BlockingType

# Non-blocking task (agent continues):
client.create_and_start_task(..., blocking_type=BlockingType.NON_BLOCKING)

name: Category
description: HITL task categories.

values.name: REGULAR
values.description: Standard task category (default)

values.name: TOOL_APPROVAL
values.description: Task for approving tool usage

name: Visibility
description: Artifact visibility types. Controls who can access the artifact.

values.name: INTERNAL
values.description: Artifact visible only within the agent system (default for upload_artifact)

values.name: EXTERNAL
values.description: Artifact visible to external consumers

name: HitlTask
description: Represents a HITL task returned from the API. Returned by get_task(), wait_for_human_submission(), and poll_until_status().

fields.name: human_artifact
fields.description: Reference to the human's submitted artifact. Only populated when status is SUBMITTED or later.

example:
task = client.wait_for_human_submission(task_id)
assert task.hitl_task_status == HitlTaskStatus.SUBMITTED
artifact_id = task.human_artifact.artifact_id

name: HitlTaskArtifact
description: Reference to a HITL artifact. Used in HitlTask.human_artifact and as input to create_and_start_task.

fields.name: artifact_id
fields.description: The unique artifact ID. Use with create_artifact_download_url to get content.

name: RequestContext
description: Context for HITL requests with snake_case interface. All four fields come from agent environment variables set by ATX platform.

methods.name: to_dict
methods.description: Convert to camelCase dict for API calls: {'jobMetadata': {'jobId': ..., 'workspaceId': ...}, 'agentInstanceId': ..., 'authorizationToken': ...}

methods.name: from_dict
methods.description: Class method. Create from camelCase API dict format.

example:
import os
from elastic_gumby_hitl_component_python_sdk import RequestContext

request_context = RequestContext(
    job_id=os.environ['JOB_ID'],
    workspace_id=os.environ['WORKSPACE_ID'],
    agent_instance_id=os.environ['AGENT_INSTANCE_ID'],
    authorization_token=os.environ['AUTHORIZATION_TOKEN'],
)

name: RequestContextTypeDef
description: TypedDict for request context in camelCase format. Alternative to RequestContext dataclass when you already have the dict.

name: serialize
description: Serialize a Pydantic model to request artifact JSON with {"properties": {...}} wrapper. This wrapper is REQUIRED by the HITL Render Engine for request artifacts (agent -> UI).

parameters.name: artifact
parameters.description: Pydantic model from elastic_gumby_hitl_component_python_sdk.types (e.g. TextInput, AutoForm, MarkdownRendererComponent)

raises.description: If artifact is None or serialization fails

example:
from elastic_gumby_hitl_component_python_sdk import serialize
from elastic_gumby_hitl_component_python_sdk.types import TextInput

artifact = TextInput(label='Enter name', value='John Doe')
json_str = serialize(artifact)
# Result: {"properties": {"label": "Enter name", "value": "John Doe"}}

name: deserialize
description: Deserialize human artifact JSON response. Human artifacts are raw JSON returned by the UI when the user submits. They do NOT have the {"properties": {...}} wrapper. Checks for QT_REFRESH flag to detect refresh requests.

parameters.name: json_str
parameters.description: Raw JSON string from human artifact (downloaded via ArtifactHelper or create_artifact_download_url)

returns.description: (data_dict, should_refresh). should_refresh is True when QT_REFRESH flag is set, meaning user clicked 'Request Changes'.

raises.description: If JSON string is null, empty, or invalid JSON

example:
from elastic_gumby_hitl_component_python_sdk import deserialize, ClosureType

data, should_refresh = deserialize(artifact_content)
if should_refresh:
    client.close_task(task_id, ClosureType.CLOSED_PENDING_NEXT_TASK)
    # Create next task with updated data
else:
    client.close_task(task_id, ClosureType.CLOSED)
    selected_option = data.get('selected_option')

name: to_hitl_json
description: Convert a dict or Pydantic model to request artifact JSON with {"properties": {...}} wrapper. Fallback for when a Pydantic type model is not available. Prefer serialize() when possible.
signature: to_hitl_json(data: dict | BaseModel, *, indent: int | None = None) -> str

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Dict or Pydantic model to serialize

parameters.name: indent
parameters.description: Optional indentation for pretty-printing

raises.description: If data is None or serialization fails

example:
from elastic_gumby_hitl_component_python_sdk.util import to_hitl_json

# When you have a raw dict instead of a Pydantic model:
json_str = to_hitl_json({'label': 'Name', 'value': 'data'})
# Result: {"properties": {"label": "Name", "value": "data"}}

name: wrap_properties
description: Wrap data in the required {"properties": {...}} structure without JSON serialization. Returns a dict, not a string.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Dict or Pydantic model to wrap

returns.description: Dict with {"properties": <data>} structure. Pydantic models are dumped with by_alias=True, exclude_none=True.

name: SerializationError
description: Raised when serialization or deserialization fails. Thrown by serialize(), deserialize(), and to_hitl_json() when given None, empty, or invalid input.

name: ArtifactHelper
description: Downloads content from presigned S3 URLs. Used internally by execute_with_refresh() but can also be used directly. Uses streaming downloads with S3-compatible headers. The 120-second default timeout accommodates FileUploadComponent responses which can be large (base64-encoded file content).

parameters.name: timeout_seconds
parameters.description: HTTP request timeout in seconds

methods.name: download
methods.signature: download(presigned_url: str, headers: Mapping[str, list[str]]) -> str
methods.description: Download artifact content as UTF-8 string. Raises requests.RequestException on failure.

example:
from elastic_gumby_hitl_component_python_sdk.util import ArtifactHelper

helper = ArtifactHelper(timeout_seconds=120)

# Get download URL from API:
download_response = agentic_client.create_artifact_download_url(
    requestContext=request_context.to_dict(),
    artifactId=task.human_artifact.artifact_id,
    visibility='INTERNAL',
)
content = helper.download(
    download_response['s3preSignedUrl'],
    download_response['requestHeaders'],
)

name: AgenticClientProtocol
description: Protocol defining the boto3 ElasticGumbyAgenticService client methods used by HitlClient. Any object implementing these methods can be passed as agentic_client.

methods.name: create_hitl_task
methods.signature: create_hitl_task(requestContext, uxComponentId, title, description, hitlRequestArtifact, **kwargs) -> dict

name: TextInput
description: A text input field component. Use with ux_component_id='TextInput'.

fields.name: label
fields.description: The label text for the input field

fields.name: value
fields.description: The initial value of the input field

fields.name: description
fields.description: Description text displayed below the label

fields.name: placeholder
fields.description: Placeholder text when field is empty

fields.name: maxLength
fields.description: Maximum allowed length of the input value

fields.name: errorAlert
fields.description: Error alert with header and optional description

example:
from elastic_gumby_hitl_component_python_sdk.types import TextInput
from elastic_gumby_hitl_component_python_sdk import serialize

artifact = TextInput(label='Enter your name', value='', placeholder='John Doe')
json_str = serialize(artifact)

name: AutoForm
description: A dynamic form generator component that renders flexible forms using CloudScape components. Supports field types: text, textarea, select, radiogroup, multiselect, checkbox, infocontainer (display-only), jsonBlock, fileUpload, fileUploadV2. Use with ux_component_id='AutoForm'.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Optional form title displayed at the top

fields.name: description
fields.description: Optional form description below the title

fields.name: fields
fields.description: Array of form fields to render in order. Each field has 'name' (identifier), 'label', 'type', and type-specific properties.

fields.name: alerts
fields.description: Optional alerts (info/warning/error/success) displayed above the form

field_types.description: Single-line text input. Supports validation (pattern, minLength, maxLength), placeholder, variant ('text'|'number').
Multi-line text input. Supports rows (default 3), validation, placeholder.
Dropdown selection. Requires options array [{label, value}]. Supports filteringType ('auto'|'none').
Radio button group for single selection. Requires options array [{label, value, description?, disabled?}].
Multi-select dropdown. Requires options array. Supports enableSelectAll, filteringType.
Display-only key-value pairs. Requires category='display', items array [{label, value}]. Supports columns (1-4), variant ('default'|'stacked').
JSON input with validation on blur. Supports rows (default 6), placeholder.
File upload (legacy). Supports maxFileSize, allowedFileTypes, multiple.
File upload with progress tracking and artifact management. Supports validation (maxFiles, maxFileSize, acceptedFileTypes).

example:
from elastic_gumby_hitl_component_python_sdk.types import AutoForm

form = AutoForm(
    title='Server Configuration',
    fields=[
        {'name': 'hostname', 'label': 'Hostname', 'type': 'text', 'required': True},
        {'name': 'region', 'label': 'Region', 'type': 'select', 'options': [
            {'label': 'US East', 'value': 'us-east-1'},
            {'label': 'US West', 'value': 'us-west-2'},
        ]},
        {'name': 'confirm', 'label': 'I confirm these settings', 'type': 'checkbox'},
    ],
)

name: MarkdownRendererComponent
description: A component for rendering Markdown content with GitHub Flavored Markdown support. Use with ux_component_id='MarkdownRendererComponent'. Commonly used for read-only displays and dashboards.

fields.name: content
fields.description: The markdown content to render (GFM supported)

fields.name: submitButtonText
fields.description: Custom text for the submit button

fields.name: hideSubmitButton
fields.description: Whether to hide the submit button (default false)

example:
from elastic_gumby_hitl_component_python_sdk.types import MarkdownRendererComponent

artifact = MarkdownRendererComponent(
    content='# Migration Plan\n\n| Step | Status |\n|------|--------|\n| Backup | Done |',
    hideSubmitButton=True,
)

name: TableComponent
description: A table component with column definitions, data items, filtering, pagination, and expandable rows. Supports column types: text (default), status-indicator, link, select (inline editable). Use with ux_component_id='TableComponent'.

fields.name: header
fields.description: The header text for the table

fields.name: columnDefinitions
fields.description: Array of column definitions with 'header', 'field', optional 'type' and 'editConfig'

fields.name: items
fields.description: Array of data items. Each must have 'id'. Optional 'parentId' for expandable rows.

fields.name: submitButtonControl
fields.description: Control submit button visibility and text

example:
from elastic_gumby_hitl_component_python_sdk.types import TableComponent

table = TableComponent(
    header='Server Inventory',
    columnDefinitions=[
        {'header': 'Name', 'field': 'name'},
        {'header': 'Status', 'field': 'status', 'type': 'status-indicator'},
    ],
    items=[
        {'id': '1', 'name': 'web-01', 'status': {'variant': 'success', 'text': 'Running'}},
        {'id': '2', 'name': 'web-02', 'status': {'variant': 'error', 'text': 'Down'}},
    ],
)

name: GeneralConnector
description: Component for selecting existing connectors or creating new ones. Use with ux_component_id='GeneralConnector' (NOT 'DynamicHITLRenderEngine').

fields.name: connector
fields.description: Connector config with connectorType ('owner|shortName|version'), maxInstances, optional title/description/fields

fields.name: maxInstances
fields.description: Maximum number of connectors of this type

fields.name: fields
fields.description: Input fields to collect per connector instance

name: FileUploadComponent
description: File upload component with validation and error handling. Saves data as {uploadedFiles: FileData[]} with base64 encoded content. Use with ux_component_id='FileUploadComponent'.

fields.name: label
fields.description: Label text for the upload component

fields.name: allowedFileTypes
fields.description: Allowed extensions without dots (e.g. ['pdf', 'doc']). Empty array allows all.

name: SelectDropdownComponent
description: Dropdown selection component with single or multiple selection. Use with ux_component_id='SelectDropdownComponent'.

name: FeedbackInput
description: A feedback input component. Use with ux_component_id='FeedbackInput'.

fields.name: label
fields.description: Label text for the feedback field

fields.name: description
fields.description: Description text for the feedback field

name: InformationalMessageComponent
description: Component for displaying informational messages. Use with ux_component_id='InformationalMessageComponent'.

name: Available UX Component Types
description: All available ux_component_id values for create_and_start_task. Generic components are defined as Pydantic models in elastic_gumby_hitl_component_python_sdk.types. Domain-specific components are also available.

special_components.name: DynamicHITLRenderEngine
special_components.description: Renders arbitrary domTreeJson UI definitions. Use get_hitl_generation_prompt() MCP tool for generation rules.
